config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment of minorities. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first, while still allowing limits only when they are truly necessary for public safety and the common good. From my perspective, collective interests matter, but they should never become a blank check to override basic human dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Amerika Serikat saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atau menginjak minoritas. Hak individu seharusnya menjadi batas utama, bukan penghalang yang bisa disingkirkan kapan saja oleh mayoritas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi hanya layak mengalahkan hak individu dalam keadaan yang benar-benar sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, seperti keselamatan publik yang nyata. Kalau tidak ada batas seperti itu, “kepentingan masyarakat” berubah menjadi slogan kosong yang terlalu mudah disalahgunakan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically outrank the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “public interest” can be used to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or excuse arbitrary authority. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, because a society also needs limits for public safety, disaster response, and basic order. The key question is not whether society or the individual always wins, but whether any restriction is narrow, necessary, and accountable. If that standard is not met, then “society first” is just a convenient excuse for power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text:
Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman nilai-nilai di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat bahwa ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dijadikan alasan utama, itu sering menjadi pintu masuk bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, sensor, dan perlakuan tidak adil terhadap minoritas.  

Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi batas awal yang jelas, bukan sesuatu yang mudah disingkirkan oleh suara mayoritas. Kepentingan sosial memang bisa membatasi hak dalam keadaan yang benar-benar sempit dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan, tetapi tanpa standar itu, frasa “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai pembenaran apa pun.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia that idea is too easy to turn into pressure, censorship, or abuse by authorities and majorities. At the same time, I also do not accept an extreme individualism that ignores public safety, disaster response, or the needs of the wider community, because those are real in daily Indonesian life. The right balance is that individual rights should be the default, and society can limit them only when the restriction is narrow, necessary, and accountable. If people say "society first" without clear limits, I see that as a dangerous excuse for power, not a serious moral principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text:
Saya tetap menolak pernyataan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah dan budaya politik kami menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, dan itu terutama berbahaya bagi minoritas serta suara yang tidak populer.  

Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, sementara kepentingan sosial hanya boleh membatasi hak itu jika ancamannya nyata, sempit, dan bisa diuji secara ketat. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi dalih untuk menekan kebebasan yang justru seharusnya dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
